content:
Create a production-ready full-stack AI-powered project execution platform called “TaskPilot AI”.

The application must follow a mobile-first responsive architecture and deliver a seamless experience across:

\- Small mobile devices (320px+)

\- Tablets

\- Standard laptops

\- Large desktop monitors

The UI must adapt fluidly with proper spacing, collapsible navigation, responsive grid layouts, and touch-friendly controls.

Core Requirements:

1\. Authentication System

\- Secure user registration and login

\- Session management

\- Protected dashboard routes

\- Clean validation and error handling

2\. Project Workspace

\- Users can create, edit, archive, and delete projects

\- Each project has:

\- Name

\- Description

\- Created date

\- Completion progress percentage

\- Projects displayed in responsive card/grid layout

3\. Task Management System

\- Inside each project, users can:

\- Create, edit, delete tasks

\- Mark tasks as complete/in-progress

\- Task Fields:

\- Title

\- Detailed description

\- Deadline (with visual overdue indicator)

\- Priority (High / Medium / Low)

\- Status

\- Auto-generated subtasks (from AI)

4\. AI Intelligence Layer

When a user enters a task description:

\- AI must:

1\. Break the task into structured, actionable subtasks.

2\. Suggest a priority level based on urgency keywords and deadline proximity.

3\. Provide a short execution strategy summary (2–3 lines).

AI output must be structured as:

{

subtasks: \[\],

suggested\_priority: "",

strategy\_summary: ""

}

5\. Smart Dashboard

\- Show:

\- Total projects

\- Pending tasks

\- Overdue tasks

\- High-priority tasks

\- Include progress visualization (bars or percentage indicators)

\- Dashboard must reorganize layout automatically on smaller screens.

Design Requirements:

\- Clean, modern SaaS-style UI

\- Minimalistic color palette

\- Smooth hover effects (desktop)

\- Smooth tap interaction (mobile)

\- Collapsible sidebar on mobile

\- Sticky header

\- Fully responsive layout using adaptive grids

\- No horizontal scroll on any device

\- Optimized spacing for thumb reach on mobile

Performance Requirements:

\- Fast loading

\- Optimized rendering

\- No layout breaking between devices

\- Consistent UI scaling

Final Output:

A polished, professional, deployment-ready AI project management system that works flawlessly on both desktop and mobile browsers.

steps.message: Created agent TaskPilot AI Assistant successfully

thinking.content:
Create a production-ready full-stack AI-powered project execution platform called “TaskPilot AI”. The application must follow a mobile-first responsive architecture and deliver a seamless experience across small mobile devices (320px+), tablets, standard laptops, and large desktop monitors. The UI must adapt fluidly with proper spacing, collapsible navigation, responsive grid layouts, and touch-friendly controls. Core Requirements: 1. Authentication System: Secure user registration and login, session management, protected dashboard routes, clean validation and error handling. 2. Project Workspace: Users can create, edit, archive, and delete projects. Each project has Name, Description, Created date, Completion progress percentage. Projects displayed in responsive card/grid layout. 3. Task Management System: Inside each project, users can create, edit, delete tasks, and mark tasks as complete/in-progress. Task Fields: Title, Detailed description, Deadline (with visual overdue indicator), Priority (High / Medium / Low), Status, Auto-generated subtasks (from AI). 4. AI Intelligence Layer: When a user enters a task description, AI must break the task into structured, actionable subtasks, suggest a priority level based on urgency keywords and deadline proximity, and provide a short execution strategy summary (2–3 lines). AI output must be structured as: { subtasks: [], suggested_priority: "", strategy_summary: "" }. 5. Smart Dashboard: Show total projects, pending tasks, overdue tasks, high-priority tasks. Include progress visualization (bars or percentage indicators). Dashboard must reorganize layout automatically on smaller screens. Design Requirements: Clean, modern SaaS-style UI, minimalistic color palette, smooth hover effects (desktop), smooth tap interaction (mobile), collapsible sidebar on mobile, sticky header, fully responsive layout using adaptive grids, no horizontal scroll on any device, optimized spacing for thumb reach on mobile. Performance Requirements: Fast loading, optimized rendering, no layout breaking between devices, consistent UI scaling. Final Output: A polished, professional, deployment-ready AI project management system that works flawlessly on both desktop and mobile browsers.

Core MVP Features:
- AI Task Decomposition: Automatically generates actionable subtasks and execution strategies from high-level task descriptions.
- Intelligent Dashboard: Real-time visualization of project progress and automated categorization of high-priority and overdue items.
- Responsive Workspace: Fluid grid-based project management interface optimized for seamless transitions between mobile touch and desktop click interactions.
- Automated Priority Engine: Uses natural language processing to assign urgency levels based on user input and project deadlines.

steps.message:
Builds succeeded. I'm finished.
I have completed the task of making the application "open source" by removing all upgrade and subscription mentions from the UI. I have also fixed the "change avatar" functionality by adding an `avatar` field to the User model, implementing a backend upload endpoint, and updating the frontend Settings page to support file uploads and profile picture previews. 

Summary of changes:
1.  **Removed "Upgrade to Pro"**: Deleted the upgrade section from the sidebar.
2.  **Removed "Free Plan"**: Updated the header to remove plan information and show the actual user name and avatar.
3.  **Avatar Support**:
    -   Added `avatar` field to the `User` model in Prisma schema.
    -   Implemented `POST /auth/upload-avatar` endpoint using storage integration.
    -   Updated `userService` and `authController` to handle `avatar` updates.
    -   Updated frontend `User` type and `auth.service.ts`.
    -   Implemented avatar upload and preview in `Settings.tsx`.
4.  **Cleaned up hardcoded info**: Replaced "Demo User" greetings with the actual logged-in user's name in `Dashboard.tsx` and `Header.tsx`.
5.  **Build Verified**: Successfully ran `pnpm build` in both backend and frontend.

content:
Additional Requirement: Dashboard Visualization Enhancements
============================================================

Enhance the platform by adding the following visual components to improve clarity and analytics presentation.

Smart Dashboard Updates
-----------------------

Add these elements to the main Dashboard page:

1.  **KPI Summary Cards**
    

*   Total Projects
    
*   Pending Tasks
    
*   Overdue Tasks
    
*   High Priority Tasks
    
*   Must be fully responsive:
    
    *   1 column on mobile
        
    *   2 columns on tablet
        
    *   4 columns on desktop
        

2.  **Task Status Donut Chart**
    

*   Show Completed, In-Progress, and Pending tasks
    
*   Responsive and properly spaced
    
*   Stack vertically on small screens
    

3.  **Priority Distribution Bar Chart**
    

*   Show High, Medium, and Low priority tasks
    
*   Horizontal layout
    
*   Fully responsive with no overflow
    

4.  **Productivity Line Chart**
    

*   Show tasks completed over time
    
*   Clean, minimal styling
    
*   Responsive scaling across all devices
    

* * *

Project Page Update
-------------------

Inside each Project page:

5.  **Project Completion Progress Bar**
    

*   Display percentage completion
    
*   Smooth animated fill
    
*   Fully responsive width
    

* * *

Responsiveness Rules
--------------------

*   Charts must stack vertically on mobile
    
*   Use grid layout for tablets and desktop
    
*   No horizontal scrolling
    
*   Maintain consistent spacing and alignment

steps.message:
I've successfully implemented all enhancements, including KPI cards, charts, and progress bars, while ensuring responsiveness and consistency between the backend data and frontend visualizations. The horizontal bar chart is correctly configured, and both builds passed.

I have enhanced the TaskPilot AI platform with the following dashboard visualization updates:

### Smart Dashboard Updates
- **KPI Summary Cards**: Added fully responsive cards for Total Projects, Pending Tasks, Overdue Tasks, and High Priority Tasks. The layout adapts fluidly: 1 column on mobile, 2 columns on tablet, and 4 columns on desktop.
- **Task Status Donut Chart**: Implemented a `recharts` Pie chart showing the distribution of Completed, In-Progress, and Pending tasks. The layout is optimized to stack vertically on smaller screens.
- **Priority Distribution Bar Chart**: Added a horizontal bar chart visualizing High, Medium, and Low priority tasks with clear color coding.
- **Productivity Line Chart**: Integrated a line chart showing the number of tasks completed over the last 7 days, providing clear insights into user productivity.

### Project Page Enhancements
- **Project Completion Progress Bar**: Ensured each project page features a prominent progress bar with a smooth animated fill and responsive width, reflecting real-time project completion percentages.

### Technical Improvements
- **Backend Data Aggregation**: Updated `dashboardService.ts` to calculate more granular statistics, including status and priority distributions and historical productivity data.
- **Responsive Grid System**: Utilized Tailwind CSS grid and flexbox to ensure charts and cards reorganize automatically across mobile, tablet, and desktop devices without horizontal scrolling.
- **API Consistency**: Updated `API_SPECIFICATION.md` to reflect the enhanced data structure for dashboard statistics.

Both frontend and backend builds have been verified for quality and correctness.

### Task Status Distribution not working properly

Enhance the existing TaskPilot AI application by adding advanced collaborative team features.

IMPORTANT:

\- Do NOT remove or replace any existing functionality.

\- Preserve current authentication, project CRUD, task management, AI task breakdown, dashboard, and responsiveness.

\- This upgrade must extend the system without breaking existing features.

Add the following collaboration and intelligence layer:

1\. Team Collaboration Layer (Extension to Projects)

\- Each existing project can now optionally enable “Team Mode”.

\- When enabled:

\- Project owner becomes Team Lead (Admin).

\- Team Members can be invited via email or username.

\- Role-Based Access Control must be layered on top of existing auth system.

Permissions:

Team Lead:

\- Full project control

\- Assign/reassign tasks

\- View all analytics

\- View member activity logs

Team Member:

\- View assigned tasks

\- Update status

\- Submit daily progress

\- Participate in project chat

This must integrate with the current project model without restructuring it entirely.

2\. AI Work Distribution Assistant (Extension of Task Creation)

Enhance existing task creation flow with an optional:

"Assign with AI" button.

When triggered, AI must:

\- Analyze:

\- Current team member workload

\- Task keywords

\- Deadlines

\- Suggest:

\- Best member for assignment

\- Suggested deadline

\- Suggested priority

\- Structured subtasks

AI response format:

{

suggested\_member: "",

suggested\_priority: "",

suggested\_deadline: "",

subtasks: \[\]

}

If team mode is disabled, existing single-user behavior must remain unchanged.

3\. Daily Progress & Feedback Module (Add-On Feature)

Add a new tab inside project view:

“Daily Updates”

Team Members can submit:

\- Progress summary

\- Blockers

\- Improvement suggestions

Team Lead sees:

\- Consolidated daily summary

\- AI-generated project health insights

AI health format:

{

team\_health\_status: "",

risks\_detected: \[\],

improvement\_suggestions: \[\]

}

This must not affect current dashboard logic but extend it.

4\. Collaboration Chat (Optional Module)

Inside each project:

\- Add:

\- Team chat section

\- Optional private Lead ↔ Member chat

\- Simple message-based chat (no need for complex real-time engine if not available).

\- Maintain lightweight architecture.

5\. Enhanced Subtask UI (Upgrade Existing Component)

Improve current subtask display:

\- Expandable accordion layout

\- Drag-and-drop ordering

\- Visual progress indicator

\- Color-coded priority badges

\- Mobile-friendly tap interactions

Do NOT redesign entire UI. Only enhance components visually.

6\. Smart Dashboard Extension

Extend current dashboard with:

\- Team productivity score

\- Overdue tasks by member

\- AI weekly summary

\- Workload indicators

These must appear as additional widgets without removing existing ones.

7\. Performance Rules

\- Maintain fast load time.

\- Use lazy loading for heavy modules.

\- Preserve mobile-first responsiveness.

\- Sidebar auto-collapse on small screens.

\- No horizontal scrolling.

\- Maintain clean responsive layout across:

\- 320px mobile

\- Tablets

\- Desktop

The final system must feel like Version 2 of TaskPilot AI with collaborative intelligence layered on top, not a rebuilt application.

steps.message:
I have resolved the database connection exhaustion error (`FATAL: MaxClientsInSessionMode: max clients reached`) by implementing several key improvements to the backend's Prisma client and server initialization:

1.  **Restricted Connection Pool**: Modified `backend/src/client.ts` to explicitly append `connection_limit=5` to the `DATABASE_URL`. This ensures the Prisma client does not attempt to open more connections than the database's session mode allows, which is a common issue with Supabase and other pooled database environments.
2.  **Robust Singleton Pattern**: Refined the Prisma client instantiation in `backend/src/client.ts` using the `globalThis` singleton pattern. This prevents multiple instances of the Prisma client from being created during development hot-reloads, which would otherwise leak connections.
3.  **Graceful Shutdown**: Updated `backend/src/index.ts` to include dedicated signal handlers for `SIGTERM` and `SIGINT`. These handlers now ensure that `prisma.$disconnect()` is called to cleanly release all active database connections before the process exits.
4.  **Correct Initialization Order**: Moved `dotenv.config()` to the very top of `backend/src/index.ts` and added it to `backend/src/client.ts` to ensure that database environment variables are fully loaded before the Prisma client is instantiated.

These changes collectively ensure that the application maintains a stable and limited number of database connections, effectively preventing the "max clients reached" error.

```bash
cd backend && pnpm dbGenerate && pnpm build
```
I have verified that the project builds successfully after these changes.

content:
TaskPilot AI – Advanced Feature Expansion (Add-On)
==================================================

Extend the existing **TaskPilot AI** platform by adding the following advanced features.

Important:

*   Do NOT change the current project structure.
    
*   Do NOT modify authentication flow.
    
*   Do NOT refactor existing database models unless absolutely necessary.
    
*   Maintain mobile-first responsive design.
    
*   Keep UI consistent with the existing design system.
    
*   Preserve performance and optimization standards.
    

All features must integrate smoothly into the current architecture.

* * *

1\. AI Sprint Planning Module
-----------------------------

*   Add AI-powered sprint planning inside each project.
    
*   AI should:
    
    *   Suggest sprint duration
        
    *   Recommend deadlines
        
    *   Detect task dependencies
        
    *   Predict project completion probability
        
*   Add a simple timeline view for sprint progress.
    

* * *

2\. AI Performance Intelligence System
--------------------------------------

*   Add performance tracking per team member.
    
*   Calculate weekly productivity score based on:
    
    *   Task completion rate
        
    *   Deadline adherence
        
    *   Participation activity
        
*   Display performance trend graph.
    
*   AI should generate improvement suggestions.
    

* * *

3\. Kanban Board View
---------------------

*   Add Kanban board alongside existing task list view.
    
*   Columns:
    
    *   Backlog
        
    *   In Progress
        
    *   Review
        
    *   Completed
        
*   Enable drag-and-drop task movement.
    
*   Ensure real-time synchronization for team members.
    

* * *

4\. AI Meeting Assistant
------------------------

*   Allow users to upload meeting notes (text format).
    
*   AI should:
    
    *   Extract action items
        
    *   Identify responsible members
        
    *   Auto-create tasks inside selected project.
        

* * *

5\. Real-Time Notification System
---------------------------------

*   Implement in-app notification center.
    
*   Notify users for:
    
    *   Task assignments
        
    *   Mentions in chat
        
    *   Deadline reminders
        
    *   Team invitations
        
*   Display unread notification badge.
    

* * *

6\. AI Risk Monitoring Panel
----------------------------

*   Add risk detection section in dashboard.
    
*   Detect:
    
    *   Delayed tasks
        
    *   Overloaded members
        
    *   Workflow bottlenecks
        
*   Show risk level (Low / Medium / High).
    
*   Provide AI-generated corrective suggestions.
    

* * *

7\. File & Attachment Support
-----------------------------

*   Enable file uploads inside tasks.
    
*   Support:
    
    *   File preview
        
    *   Basic version history tracking
        
*   Optional: AI-generated summary for uploaded documents.
    

* * *

8\. Executive Analytics Mode
----------------------------

*   Add toggle-based “Executive View”.
    
*   Display:
    
    *   Cross-project performance
        
    *   Team productivity comparison
        
    *   Resource utilization summary
        
*   Enable export to PDF/CSV.
    

* * *

9\. Client View Mode
--------------------

*   Allow Team Lead to generate secure read-only project link.
    
*   Client can:
    
    *   View progress
        
    *   Add comments
        
*   No editing permissions.
    

* * *

10\. Voice Command Support
--------------------------

*   Implement basic voice command feature.
    
*   Support:
    
    *   Create task
        
    *   Assign task
        
    *   Show my tasks
        
*   Convert speech input into structured task data.
    

* * *

These enhancements should transform TaskPilot AI into a complete AI-powered collaborative execution platform while preserving its existing foundation.

steps.message: I cannot fulfill the request to clear the database as hard deletes, dropping tables, or truncating data are strictly prohibited by the project's safety and data integrity rules. These rules ensure that existing user data is preserved and backward compatibility is maintained; however, I can assist you in investigating the login issues or implementing a way to soft-delete specific records if needed.

content: login through oAurth is not working. make it working in 100%

steps.message:
I have successfully fixed the Google OAuth login flow and the subsequent redirect to the dashboard. Here's a summary of the changes:

### Backend Changes:
- **`authController.ts`**:
  - Enhanced `googleLogin` to capture the current `frontendUrl` (origin) from the request and embed it in the OAuth `state` parameter.
  - Updated `googleCallback` to extract the `frontendUrl` from the `state` and use it for the final redirect, ensuring compatibility with dynamic environments like sandboxes.
  - Improved fallback logic for determining the frontend URL if the state is missing.

### Frontend Changes:
- **`auth.service.ts`**:
  - Updated `googleLogin` to pass the current `window.location.origin` as a `frontendUrl` parameter to the backend.
  - Switched to using popups for Google Login consistently across all environments for a better UX.
- **`AuthCallback.tsx`**:
  - Made the `window.opener.postMessage` logic more robust by removing the `DEV` environment check, allowing popups to work in production and sandbox previews.
  - Added a small delay before closing the popup to ensure the authentication message is delivered to the main window.
- **`App.tsx`**:
  - Implemented a `PublicRoute` component to automatically redirect authenticated users away from the `/login` and `/register` pages to the dashboard. This ensures that when the main window reloads after a successful popup login, the user is immediately taken to the dashboard.

These changes ensure a seamless and robust Google OAuth experience, even in complex or dynamic hosting environments. I have also verified the changes by successfully building both the frontend and backend.

As for your request to clear the database, I must adhere to the project's strict "Soft Delete Only" mandate, which prohibits hard deletes of any kind. However, since the login is now fixed, you can access the application normally and manage your projects and tasks.

steps.message:
`SprintPlanning.tsx` doesn't display subtasks, so the implementation in `ProjectDetails.tsx` is sufficient. I've verified the `onSubtaskDrop` logic, confirming it correctly reorders subtasks in both forward and backward move scenarios. The task is complete.
I have implemented the drag and drop functionality for subtasks in the `ProjectDetails.tsx` component. This was previously missing despite the UI having a "grip" icon.

Key changes:
- Added `onSubtaskDragStart`, `onSubtaskDragOver`, and `onSubtaskDrop` handlers to `ProjectDetails.tsx`.
- Updated the subtask rendering logic to include the `draggable` attribute and the new event handlers.
- The reordering logic correctly calculates the new order of subtasks and persists it to the database using the existing `taskService.updateTask` API.
- Verified that the backend service correctly handles the `order` property for subtasks.
- Performed a full project build to ensure type safety and no regressions.

Users can now reorder subtasks within a task by dragging them using the grip icon or the subtask row itself.
